items.title: Split-apply-combine with groupby
items.mermaid:
flowchart TD
  raw["input DataFrame"] --> split["split by group keys"]
  split --> g1["group A rows"]
  split --> g2["group B rows"]
  g1 --> apply1["apply agg or transform"]
  g2 --> apply2["apply agg or transform"]
  apply1 --> combine["combine results"]
  apply2 --> combine
  combine --> outAgg["agg: one row per group"]
  combine --> outXform["transform: same row count"]
items.caption: groupby splits rows by key, applies a function per group, then combines—either collapsing (agg) or broadcasting back (transform).

items.title: Left merge preserves all left rows
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  leftTbl["left table"] --> mergeOp["pd.merge how=left"]
  rightTbl["right table"] --> mergeOp
  mergeOp --> matched["matched keys: fill columns"]
  mergeOp --> unmatched["unmatched left: NaN in right cols"]
items.caption: A left join keeps every left row; missing right-side keys produce NaN instead of dropping the left row (unlike inner join).

items.title: Leaky vs safe group-feature pipeline
items.mermaid:
flowchart TD
  full["full dataset"] --> badPath["groupby on ALL rows"]
  badPath --> badJoin["merge stats back"] --> badSplit["train_test_split"] --> leaky["inflated metrics"]
  full --> goodSplit["split first"]
  goodSplit --> trainOnly["groupby on TRAIN only"]
  trainOnly --> safeMap["map/merge onto train and test"] --> safeModel["train model"]
items.caption: Computing group statistics before splitting leaks test information into features; split first, aggregate on train, then map to all partitions.
